construct.en: Current level of depletion in regulatory resources
construct.tr: Düzenleyici kaynaklarda o anki tükenme düzeyi

original_citation.en: Johnson, R. E., Lanaj, K., & Barnes, C. M. (2014). The good and bad of being fair: Effects of procedural and interpersonal justice behaviors on regulatory resources. Journal of Applied Psychology, 99(4), 635–650. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0035647
original_citation.tr: Johnson, R. E., Lanaj, K., & Barnes, C. M. (2014). The good and bad of being fair: Effects of procedural and interpersonal justice behaviors on regulatory resources. Journal of Applied Psychology, 99(4), 635–650. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0035647

description.en: A 5-item state regulatory depletion scale used by Johnson et al. (2014) to assess short-term depletion.
description.tr: Johnson vd. (2014) tarafından durumluk tükenmeyi değerlendirmek için kullanılan 5 maddeli ölçek.

response_format_tr: 7’li Likert tipi ölçek, 1 = Hiç doğru değil ile 7 = Tamamen doğru
response_format_en: 7-point Likert scale, from 1 = not at all true to 7 = completely true
target_population_tr: Tekrarlı ölçüm ya da durumluk değerlendirme bağlamındaki yetişkinler. Bu makalede üniversite öğrencisi örnekleminde test edilmiştir.
target_population_en: Adults in repeated-measures or state-assessment contexts. In this article, it was tested in a university student sample.

reliability.en: Within-person omega was .89 in the pilot study.

validity_notes.en: MCFA showed acceptable fit after one residual covariance was freed. Loadings ranged from .73 to 1.00 at the within-person level and from .63 to .94 at the between-person level.
validity_notes.tr: MCFA, bir artık kovaryansı serbest bırakıldıktan sonra kabul edilebilir uyum göstermiştir. Faktör yükleri kişi-içi düzeyde .73 ile 1.00, kişiler-arası düzeyde .63 ile .94 arasında değişmiştir.

citation.en: Peker, M., & Bağlıcakoğlu, G. (2025). Task-related fluctuations in action-state orientation: Roles of anticipated task difficulty and task-related affect. Motivation and Emotion, 49(2), 103-119. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11031-024-10108-8
citation.tr: Peker, M., & Bağlıcakoğlu, G. (2025). Task-related fluctuations in action-state orientation: Roles of anticipated task difficulty and task-related affect. Motivation and Emotion, 49(2), 103-119. https://doi.org/10.1007/s11031-024-10108-8
